title: Return structured HTTP response from fetchSignals and update public types

requirements.text: Update the backend public type `SignalFetchResult` in `backend/main.mo` to return a structured HTTP response payload instead of `[Signal]`, changing it to: `#ok : { statusCode : Nat; body : Text; timestamp : Int } | #err : BotError` (keeping the existing `BotError` variants unchanged).
acceptanceCriteria: `backend/main.mo` defines `public type SignalFetchResult` with `#ok` carrying a record `{ statusCode : Nat; body : Text; timestamp : Int }` and `#err : BotError`.
The canister interface (Candid) reflects the new `SignalFetchResult` shape so the frontend can compile against it.

requirements.text: In `backend/main.mo`, update `fetchSignals` so that after a successful HTTP outcall (i.e., after `response = await OutCall.httpGetRequest(...)` completes without trapping), the function returns `#ok({ statusCode = 200; body = response; timestamp = Time.now() })` instead of `#ok([])`.

quotes:
Currently line 663 returns #ok([]) after the outcall succeeds.
The variable `response` contains the HTTP response body as Text.

On success (the outcall completed without trapping):
  Return #ok({ statusCode = 200, body = response,
    timestamp = Time.now() }).

acceptanceCriteria: On the success path (valid https URL, outcall completes), `fetchSignals` returns `#ok` with `statusCode = 200`, `body = response`, and `timestamp = Time.now()`.
`fetchSignals` does not attempt to parse JSON into `Signal` records.

requirements.text: Keep the existing `fetchSignals` error behavior in `backend/main.mo` unchanged: on authorization failure, missing bot profile, missing bot URL, invalid URL, or any existing error case, continue returning the same `#err` variants and messages as currently implemented.

quotes: On any error, keep the existing #err returns.
Do not modify any other endpoints.

acceptanceCriteria: All existing `#err(...)` return paths in `fetchSignals` remain present and return the same `BotError` variants/messages as before.
No other public methods besides `fetchSignals` and the necessary public type definitions are changed.

requirements.text: In `backend/main.mo`, ensure `fetchSignals` still updates the callerŌĆÖs bot profile heartbeat timestamp exactly as it currently does (i.e., set `lastHeartbeat = Time.now()` in the stored `BotProfile`) on the successful outcall path.

quotes: Still update lastHeartbeat as currently implemented.

acceptanceCriteria: After a successful `fetchSignals` call, the stored `BotProfile.lastHeartbeat` for the caller is updated to a new `Time.now()` value as before.

requirements.text: In `backend/main.mo`, add an audit log entry on the successful outcall path of `fetchSignals` whose details include the first 100 characters of the HTTP response body (or the full body if it is <= 100 characters), using the exact prefix format: `"Signals fetched: " # <snippet>`.

quotes:
Add an audit entry that includes the first 100 characters of the
response body: "Signals fetched: " # (if response.size() > 100
then take first 100 chars else full response).

acceptanceCriteria: On success, an audit entry is added with action `FETCH_SIGNALS` (or the existing action used for fetchSignals) and details that begin with `Signals fetched: ` followed by a snippet of `response` capped at 100 characters.
If `response.size() <= 100`, the full `response` text is included after the prefix.

requirements.text: Update the frontend connectivity-test hook `frontend/src/hooks/useFetchSignalsTest.ts` to handle the new `fetchSignals` success shape (`{ statusCode; body; timestamp }`) and update the success toast message to reflect the structured result (e.g., include status code and/or body length) rather than `signals.length`.

quotes:
Update the public type definitions so the frontend can consume
the new result shape.

acceptanceCriteria: `useFetchSignalsTest` compiles with the updated `fetchSignals` return type and no longer assumes `result.ok` is an array.
On success, the toast message is in English and references the structured payload (e.g., `statusCode` and/or `body`), without referencing a signals array length.

requirements.text: Regenerate or update the frontendŌĆÖs backend interface type definitions (e.g., the generated declarations used by the actor) so TypeScript correctly reflects the updated `SignalFetchResult` Candid type and `actor.fetchSignals()` return type.

quotes:
Update the public type definitions so the frontend can consume
the new result shape.

acceptanceCriteria: Frontend TypeScript builds without type errors related to `SignalFetchResult` or `actor.fetchSignals()`.
The frontendŌĆÖs declared `SignalFetchResult` type matches the backendŌĆÖs new `#ok` record shape (`statusCode`, `body`, `timestamp`) and the existing `#err : BotError` union.

constraints: Do not modify any other endpoints besides `fetchSignals` and the necessary public type definitions required to expose the new `SignalFetchResult` shape.
Do not parse arbitrary JSON into `Signal` records in Motoko for this MVP; return the raw HTTP response body as `Text`.
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: Implementing JSON parsing of the HTTP response into `[Signal]` on the backend.
Changing the HTTP outcall mechanism (`OutCall.httpGetRequest`) or the existing `transform` function behavior.
Adding new frontend screens or UI panels beyond updating existing connectivity test behavior.
